items.title: GLM-5.2 makes open coding models credible
items.oneliner: GLM-5.2 looks like the first open-weight coding model teams might actually ship, not just benchmark and forget.

items.title: OpenAI is pushing harder into health
items.oneliner: OpenAI says 230 million weekly health queries and 18 rare-disease hits. Consumer chat is becoming clinical infrastructure.

items.title: MCP gets enterprise-managed authorization
items.oneliner: MCP now supports zero-touch enterprise auth, which removes OAuth prompt hell and makes agent tooling deployable at company scale.

items.title: Anthropic restrictions sharpen sovereignty concerns
items.oneliner: Anthropic access limits and looser disclosure language remind buyers that model access is now a geopolitical dependency.

items.title: AUR attacks exposed package trust gaps
items.oneliner: Attackers hijacked orphaned AUR packages, a blunt reminder that community package ecosystems still have supply-chain blind spots.

items.title: Brazil's emergency alert system was spoofed
items.oneliner: A fake extreme alert hit phones across Brazil, showing public warning systems are now part of the attack surface.

items.title: Job scams are getting technically sophisticated
items.oneliner: Fake hiring flows now steal identities and bypass trust instincts, which means security training needs a job-search chapter.

items.title: Cloudflare is building for agent operations
items.oneliner: Cloudflare's temporary accounts and agent reliability push target a real bottleneck: long-running agents need operational plumbing, not demos.

items.title: AI memory demand is hitting hardware prices
items.oneliner: Apple warned AI server memory demand could raise device prices, making inference economics everyone else's problem too.

items.title: Perplexity is productizing agent memory
items.oneliner: Perplexity claims 25% better correctness from a memory graph, signaling that context persistence is becoming a core product layer.
